message:
feat(search): add bold match highlighting in snippets, fix cross-session dedup, chunk progressive SQL, and raise model download body limit

Bold match highlighting in prefix/cached snippets:
- quick_prefix_snippet() and cached_prefix_snippet() now wrap the first
  query match in **bold** markdown markers. When a user searches for
  "entropy" and the content contains "calculate the entropy", the snippet
  now shows "calculate the **entropy**" instead of plain text. This makes
  it immediately obvious why a result matched, especially for long
  content where the match might otherwise be hard to spot.

Cross-session dedup fix:
- deduplicate_hits() and rrf_fuse_hits() previously keyed dedup on
  (source_id, content_hash), which incorrectly collapsed identical
  messages across different sessions within the same source. The key is
  now (source_id, source_path, content_hash), preserving results from
  different sessions that happen to contain the same content (e.g.,
  boilerplate system prompts that appear in every session). String
  interning via HashMap<String, u32> avoids the memory overhead of
  cloning source_path strings for every hit.

Tool noise filtering improvement:
- rrf_fuse_hits() now checks the snippet field as a fallback when
  content is empty, ensuring tool invocation noise is filtered even
  when the hit comes from a snippet-only path.

Snippet truncation optimization:
- snippet_from_content() now uses a single-pass iterator approach
  (chars().by_ref().take(200)) instead of counting all characters first
  and then taking 200. For long messages this avoids a redundant full
  traversal.

Progressive semantic SQL chunking:
- The progressive search SQL query that resolves semantic lookup keys
  now chunks keys into batches of 300 to avoid exceeding SQLite's
  default parameter limit (999). Each key uses 3 parameters, so batches
  of 300 use up to 900 parameters per query. Previously, a search with
  >333 unique semantic hits would produce a SQL error.

Model download body size limit:
- Raise the asupersync HTTP client max_body_size from the default 16 MiB
  to 500 MiB for model downloads. The all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model is ~86 MB,
  which exceeded the default limit and caused downloads to fail silently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
